CAPITOL OFFICE
STATE CAPITOL
SACRAMENTO, CA 95814
(916) 445-7795

COMMITTEES:
AGRICULTURE
FINANCE AND INSURANCE
WAYS AND MEANS

VICE CHAIRMAN:
RURAL CAUCUS
MEMBER:
SELECT COMMITTEE ON
SMALL BUSINESS

DISTRICT OFFICE
5323 HIGUERA STREET
SAN LUIS OBISPO, CA 93401
(805) 549-3381

SUBCOMMITTEES:
RESOURCES AND PARKS
STATE ADMINISTRATION
UNEMPLOYMENT AND
DISABILITY INSURANCE

LESLIE RAMSEY
ADMINISTRATIVE ASSISTANT

JOINT COMMITTEE:
FAIRS AND EXPOSITIONS

SANDRA COLBY
FIELD REPRESENTATIVE

Assembly
California Legislature

ERIC SEASTRAND
ASSEMBLYMAN, TWENTY-NINTH DISTRICT
ASSEMBLY REPUBLICAN WHIP

February 8, 1990


Mr. John Greenwood
Legislative Coordinator
Fair Political Practices Commission
Post Office Box 807
Sacramento, CA 95804

Dear Mr. Greenwood:

I would appreciate your assistance in providing a suggested
response to the concern of my constituent, Mr. Jim Garing of
Arroyo Grande, regarding the Commission. I have enclosed a copy
of his Casework Intake Form for your information.

Please direct your response to me at my San Luis Obispo district
office. Thank you for your assistance in this regard.

Sincerely,

ERIC SEASTRAND

ES:rl

Enclosure

[The image appears to be mostly blank/white with some very faint text that is largely illegible. The text is too faint to accurately transcribe with confidence.]

CASEWORK INTAKE FORM


Jim Garing                                           February 8, 1990
Name of Constituent                                  Date

Garing, Taylor and Assoc., Inc.                     Ruth
Business                                             Staff

141 South Elm Street
Address                                              / xx/ _____ _____
                                                     Phone    Visit    Letter
Arroyo Grande, CA  93420
City                          Zip                    489-1321
                                                     Telephone

_________________________________                    _________________________
Social Security No.                                  Claim No.


PERTINENT FACTS AND DESCRIPTION OF PROBLEM:

Mr. Garing is a small businessman doing business with the City at times.  He feels the Fair Political Practices Commission is out of hand and that the Commission is infringing on everyday ordinary businessman such as himself.  Because he does work with the City, he is required by the Commission to fill out forms, not only he, but at least 10 employees, and return them to the Commission.  He figures that this will cost him at least $1,000.  There really does not seem to be a need to require this much regulation.  Why couldn't just the president of the company be required to complete the forms?
